column3: Slug is used in the URLs on the portal
Vibrio tasmaniensis, Vibrio pomeroyi, Vibrio atlanticus
Apis mellifera (Western Honey bee), Varroa destructor (honey bee mite)
Australian tick species from the understudied subgenus Sternalixodes: Ixodes cornuatus, I. myrmecobii (the numbat tick), I. confusus, I. hirsti, and I. trichosuri (the brushtail possum tick).
Northern Hairy-nosed Wombat (Lasiorhinus krefftii); Shark Bay bandicoot (Perameles bougainville)
Macropus giganteus (Eastern grey kangaroo), Anas platyrhynchos (Wild ducks), Canis lupus familiaris (Domestic dogs)

Phascolarctos cinereus (Koala), Macropus giganteus (Eastern Grey Kangaroo), Trichosurus vulpecula (Common Brushtail Possum), Trichoglossus moluccanus (Rainbow Lorikeet), Spilopelia chinensis (Eastern Spotted Dove), Podargus strigoides (Tawny Frogmouth), Corvus orru (Torresian Crow)
Arboviruses (focusing on orthobunyaviruses, orthoflaviviruses and alphaviruses)

Seabird species from the Norfolk Island–Phillip Island system: Little Shearwater (Puffinus assimilis), Providence Petrel (Pterodroma solandri), Wedge-tailed Shearwater (Ardenna pacifica), Grey Ternlet (Anous albivitta), Common Noddy (Anous stolidus), Masked Booby (Sula dactylatra), Flesh-footed Shearwater (Ardenna carneipes), White-necked Petrel (Pterodroma cervicalis), Kermadec Petrel (Pterodroma neglecta), Red-tailed Tropicbird (Phaethon rubricauda), Sooty Tern (Onychoprion fuscatus), Black-winged Petrel (Pterodroma nigripennis), and Black Noddy (Anous minutus).

Sub-Antarctic seabird - Snowy sheathbill (Chionis albus), southern giant petrel (Macronectes giganteus), and brown skua / Antarctic skua complex (Stercorarius antarcticus / Stercorarius lonnbergi), gentoo penguin (Pygoscelis papua), king penguin (Aptenodytes patagonicus), macaroni penguin (Eudyptes chrysolophus)
Magpie Goose (Anseranas semipalmata), Plumed Whistling Duck (Dendrocygna eytoni), Radjah Shelduck (Radjah radjah)
Echinoderms - Sea stars, sea urchins, sea cucumbers
Amegilla cingulata (blue-banded bees) and Eristalis tenax (hoverflies)
